revision.text:
[[ja:日岡陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:日岡陵古墳.jpg|location={{ill|Kakogawa|qid=Q750569|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.779953|N|134.862189|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Distinguish|{{ill|Hinooka Kofun|en|Hinooka Kofun|qid=Q18338989|lt=Hioka Kofun|1=Hinooka Kofun}}|x1=Located in {{ill|Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県うきは市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Hioka Ryōkofun|ja|日岡陵古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Ryōkofun|1=Hioka Goryo Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505277}} |image = [[File:Hiokaryo Kofun haisho.JPG|280px]]
Front section frontal view
( {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima Inabihirohime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}} Hioka Ryō Worship Hall ) |alternate_name = Hioka Goryō / Hirehaka | part_of = {{ill|Hioka-yama Kofun Group|ja|日岡山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka-yama Kofun Group|1=Hiokayama Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q98492835}} |location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Kakogawa-chō Ōno |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 47.83 |coordinates = 134 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 43.88 |omitted = JP-28 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |map_size = Mound length: 80 m
Height: 7.5 m (posterior circular part) |burial = Unknown |built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |Interred = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima Inabihirohime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}} |excavations = |Mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Hioka Ryō" |notes = |map = Japan Hyōgo |map_dot_label = Hioka Ryōkofun |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Southwest|ja|前方後円墳-南西|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Southwest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Hioka Ryōkofun''' (日岡陵古墳) is a kofun located in Kakogawa-chō Ōno, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Hioka-yama Kofun Group|ja|日岡山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka-yama Kofun Group|1=Hiokayama Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q98492835}}. Although the actual interred individual remains unclear, it has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Hioka Ryō" (ひのおかのみささぎ) for the mausoleum of the 12th [[:en:Keikō Tennō|Keikō Tennō]] Empress, {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima Inabihirohime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}} (Harimano Inabihirotsumeno-mikoto). == Overview == It is a kofun constructed on the summit of Mount Hioka, at the northern edge of the Innan Plateau on the eastern bank of the Kakogawa in southern {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. In addition to the surveyed maps from 1928 (Showa 3) produced by the typeer Imperial Household Ministry{{Sfn|加古川市史 第1巻|1989|pp=225-227}}, a mound survey was carried out by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency burial Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}} in 2011 (Heisei 23){{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012|pp=37-45}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its anterior portion oriented southwest, and is almost entirely preserved{{Sfn|日岡山古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}. There were, however, two competing theories: one suggesting that it was originally a circular tumulus that was transtypeed into a keyhole type during the Meiji-period renovations, and another contending that it had not been altered{{Sfn|加古川市史 第1巻|1989|pp=225-227}}{{Sfn|日岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}. According to the 2011 (Heisei 23) survey, it appears that it was constructed as a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the beginning{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012|pp=37-45}}. The existence of terracing is uncertain (either absent or only a narrow terrace is present){{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012|pp=37-45}}. The outer surface of the mound has yielded roofing stones, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, and pieces of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012|pp=37-45}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the 4th century of the Early Kofun period, making it the oldest among the {{ill|Hioka-yama Kofun Group|ja|日岡山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka-yama Kofun Group|1=Hiokayama Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q98492835}}{{Sfn|日岡山古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}. While the identity of the interred remains archaeologically uncertain, the {{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima Kokufudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} recounts a tradition regarding the "hirehaka" (褶墓) of the consort of [[:en:Keikō Tennō|Keikō Tennō]] (referred to as Innan Wakiratsumeno-mikoto), leading the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} to designate it as the mausoleum of the Empress, {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima Inabihirohime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}}{{Sfn|日岡山古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012|pp=37-45}}: * Mound length: approximately 80 meters * Posterior circular part: ** Diameter: approximately 45 meters ** Height: approximately 7.5 meters * Anterior section: ** Width: approximately 33 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Neck (constriction) section: ** Height: approximately 4 meters == Interred == Although the actual individual interred in Hioka Ryōkofun remains uncertain, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the mausoleum of the 12th [[:en:Keikō Tennō|Keikō Tennō]] Empress, {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima Inabihirohime|lt_ja=播磨稲日大郎姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}} (Harimano Inabihirotsumeno-mikoto)宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} "Mausoleum Guidelines"] (1934, National Diet Library Digital Collection), 9 panels.{{Sfn|日岡陵（国史）}}"Mausoleum Topographical Compilation, Reduced Edition" by the burial Division of the Imperial Household Agency, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 402.. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, she is referred to as "Harima Inabihirohime" or "Harima Tarohime", while the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} refers to her as "Harinama no Inabinoo Tarohime"; moreover, the {{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima Kokufudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} presents a tradition concerning "Innan Besshō" (いなみのわきいらつめ), which some scholars consider to denote the same individual. In either case, the name "Inabi (Inami)" is derived from a place name in Harima ({{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} and {{ill|Innan District|ja|印南郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Innan District|1=Innami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11409085}}). [[File:Hioka-jinja gaihaiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505275}}}}]] Additionally, at the southern foot of Mount Hioka, the {{ill|Engishiki Naisha|ja|延喜式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|lt=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Naisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505275}} is enshrined. According to its tradition, at the time of childbirth for figures such as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|lt=Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, the deity {{ill|Ten Isasahiko|ja|天伊佐佐比古命|1=Ame-no-Isasahiko|2=ja|3=天伊佐佐比古命|lt=Ame-no-Isasahiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Isasahiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the enshrined deity of Hioka Shrine) pertypeed prayers for a safe delivery. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board * Publications by local governments ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=Kakogawa City History, Volume 1: Main Text 1|publisher=Kakogawa City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|加古川市史 第1巻|1989}}}} * Publications by the Imperial Household Agency ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo063b037.pdf "Survey of the Mound Outline of Hioka Ryō, Empress of Keikō Tennō, Harima Inabihirohime"]}}|title=Kiyō Bulletin|volume=Mausolea Section|number=No. 63|publisher={{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency burial Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第63号|2012}}}} – Link: Imperial Household Agency "Catalog of the burial Department Collection and Image Release System". * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Hioka Ryōkofun|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|en|Kokushi Daijiten|qid=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|日岡陵（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=Hioka Kofun Group|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Hioka-yama Kofun Group|title=Hyōgo Prefecture Place names 2|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}}29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490611|ref={{Harvid|日岡山古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Innan Besshō|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Personal name Dictionary|volume=Popular Edition|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|印南別嬢（古代氏族）|2010}}}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|Hioka-yama Kofun Group|ja|日岡山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka-yama Kofun Group|1=Hiokayama Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q98492835}} * {{ill|Harima Inabihirohime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabihirohime|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kako-navi.jp/spot/spot/purpose/history/896.html] – {{ill|Kakogawa Tourism Association|ja|加古川観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|日岡陵古墳|version=101753389|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日岡陵古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11505277}} [[Category:History of Kakogawa, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]
